title: lachnagrostis filiformis var. littoralis
intro: Lachnagrostis filiformis var. littoralis, also known as coastal tussock grass, is a plant species that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to the coastal regions of New Zealand and Australia. The plant is rich in flavonoids and saponins, which are believed to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of coastal tussock grass include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support immune function. Coastal tussock grass can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
